rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LISBETH INGRID PETER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/10/21. (No earlier date available.) The original bank statement must be for the ending date of the accounting period (now 10/30/20). [LRSF 14.35 D.] If the attorney of record retains the original document as with retention of original documents under LRSF 2.11 M.4., the attorney must file a Declaration certifying the retention of the original for that required amount of time. Amounts received during estate administration that were reclaimed by the agency/payor are not receipts for purposes of calculating the fee base. Was there an order authorizing the $2,823.25 in fees paid to the Public Administrator on 7/7/20? There is nothing in the records. Why are so many of the attorney costs for File&ServeXpress $11.50, not $5.00? What were the E-Service charges? (The order appointing the general personal representative was dated 3/19/19 but it was filed on 3/20/19. The claims period does not start to toll until Letters are first issued to a general personal representative: in this case, on 3/21/19.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DEL PRADO FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Dismiss Case 19-302802 In Its "Entirety"
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] Mr. Del Prado's "Peremptory Challenge to Judicial Officer: 'Ross C. Moody'" does not state the statutory authority under which it is brought. Based on the language in the document, this could have been intended an objection brought under either CCP section 170.3 or 170.6. If the Peremptory Challenge to Judge Moody is sought under CCP section 170.6, it is DENIED. A party can disqualify a judge without any reason by executing a declaration under penalty of perjury indicating the attorney or party cannot have a fair or impartial hearing before the judge (CCP section 170.6(a)(2)). However, only one such disqualification is allowed on behalf of each side in each case (CCP section 170.6(a)(4)). Mr. Del Prado, orally and in open court, entered a motion for disqualification under CCP section 170.6 as to Hon. Judge Joseph M. Quinn on August 19, 2019. Mr. Del Prado cannot bring a second motion under CCP section 170.6 in this proceeding, as he has already brought one. Further, even if Mr. Del Prado had not already moved under CCP section 170.6 in these proceedings, the disqualification statute does not apply when the challenged judge presided at an earlier hearing involving a contested fact issue relating to the merits of the case (CCP section 170.6(a)(2); Stephens v. Sup. Ct. (2002) 96 Cal. App. 4th 54, 59). Judge Moody previously granted successor trustee Bennett Hong's petition to quiet title to the Trust's real property and to declare Mr. Del Prado's attempted transfer of the property void in abnitio on September 2, 2020. The pending motion seeks to "dismiss" all proceedings in this case number. Under the continuation of previous proceeding rule, a motion under CCP section 170.6 to disqualify Judge Moody from hearing the pending motion to dismiss would be untimely. Alternatively, if the Peremptory Challenge to Judge Moody is sought under CCP section 170.3, the Court hereby strikes it as insufficient under the statute, as it fails to disclose legal grounds for disqualification on its face. A request for disqualification under CCP section 170.3 must be made "at the earliest practicable opportunity after discovery of the facts constituting the ground for disqualification" and must disclose the legal grounds for disqualification on its face. The challenged judge has authority only to determine the issue of timeliness and to strike the statement of disqualification if it is untimely or fails to disclose legal grounds for disqualification on its face. (CCP section 170.4(b)). A disqualification statement that merely contains allegations on information and belief or conclusions, or which is merely based on dissatisfaction with the judge's prior rulings, is insufficient under the statute. It shall not be disqualifying for a judge to have, "in any capacity expressed a view on a legal or factual issue presented in the proceeding [with three exceptions not applicable here]." (CCP section 170.2(b).) Rene Del Prado's "Motion to Dismiss Case 19-302802 In It's [sic] 'Entirety'" is DENIED. First, the motion is procedurally improper, as it fails to comply with the requirements of California Rules of Court, 3.1110, 3.1112, 3.1113(b), and 3.1300, as well as Code of Civil Procedure section 1005. There is Proof of Service of this motion to beneficiary and former co-trustee Kathryn Guillen and her attorney, but no proof of service to successor trustee Bennett Hong or his attorney. Second, although captioned as a "motion to dismiss," there are no grounds on which to dismiss the proceedings under Code of Civil Procedure section 583.410. At present, there are no petitions or other matters currently pending in Case PTR-19-302802, beyond this motion. In sum, there is nothing to be dismissed. Accordingly, the motion to dismiss is denied. [Part 1 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DEL PRADO FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Dismiss Case 19-302802 In Its "Entirety" (This Line Is Added To The Calendar For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Purposes Only)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] Third, to the extent that the motion may be a poorly-framed motion to compel arbitration pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.2 (based on references in the motion to an arbitration clause in the trust document), it is denied. Based on the record, the court finds that Mr. Del Prado has waived his right to arbitration of the matters the court has already heard and ruled upon in the October 22, 2019 and September 2, 2020 orders. Proceedings on these petitions concluded months ago, and he made no attempt on his part to timely oppose or appeal them, or to enforce an arbitration clause he now brings before the Court for the first time in this filing. He has engaged in unreasonable delay in seeking to compel arbitration. There has been judicial determination of the matters, of which Mr. Del Prado was given notice. Further, his attempt to transfer the Trust property to a third party for nominal consideration after he was removed as a trustee by court order, and his attempt to remove the proceedings to Bankruptcy Court are both actions inconsistent with the right to arbitrate or with an intent to invoke arbitration. (See Christensen v. Dewor Devel. (1983) 33 Cal. 3d 778, 782, St. Agnes Med. Ctr. v. PacifiCare of California (2003) 31 Cal. 4th 1187, and Davis v. Cont'l Airlines, Inc. (1997) 59 Cal. App. 4th 205). Fourth, the court further denies Mr. Del Prado's request to censure attorney Christopher Jew for his representation of Kathryn Guillen in this matter. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. January 12, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. The appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NESTEL REVOCABLE TRUST, ESTABLISHED FEBRUARY 21, 1997 AND REVOKED ON JULY 1, 2019 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) The Settlement Agreement stipulates to the effective instrument being the 1997 Trust as amended, including the 7/1/19 Trust Instrument. Paragraph B of the Recitals states that the "1997 Trust" includes the Third 2017 Confirming Amendment to the 1997 Nestel Revocable Trust. The following is noted: a. The Third 2017 Confirming Amendment attempts to amend Trust B under the 1997 Trust, even though Trust B was irrevocable at the time, by asserting that it was an exercise of a power of appointment, even though the 1997 Trust specifically required the power of appointment to be exercised by a will. b. The 2019 Trust is a completely separate trust. It is not a restatement and it is not an amendment. 2) GAL for Lyric Nestel and Rhodes Nestel has not filed a consent to the settlement agreement. 3) Petitioner requests the court order that the agreement and mutual release is fair, reasonable, and in the best of all parties. The following is noted: a. This is a finding, not an order; b. The Court generally does not make this finding; c. It is unclear whether such a finding could be made as to the Estate of Harryette Nestel as no one has been appointed personal representative and it is not a party here. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 8th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 8th amended general order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST AND LAST NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CARL TANDY

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required solely for Court to determine whether this petition should be taken off calendar for lack of progress. No argument on merits will be heard. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Please review the Court's 8th Amended General Order which took effect on 12/07/2020 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL FRANCIS MCGOVERN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to determine if the (second) amended petition should be taken off calendar for failure to cure defects. As a result of the renewed stay home order due to the COVID-19 emergency, effective 12/6/20, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 8th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEORGE AKIHIRO MAEDA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: continued to 2/10/21. still no order delivered to the examiner and no proof of service of the notice of hearing form filed. new deadline in order to avoid another automatic continuance is noon on 2/1/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES EARL JEWELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner requests admission of lost will. Declaration filed on 12/07/20 attempts to address prior Examiner's Notes regarding presumption of revocation of will. However, it does not discuss Probate Code 6124, other than to cite it, but instead discusses other code sections. The declaration also states that, due to Petitioner's health, he has been prevented from doing a full inspection of Decedent's home to locate the original will. 2) Petitioner alleges that the date of the Will is 5/11/16 but Decedent's signature is dated 5/10/16. The witnesses, who affirmed that Decedent signed in their presence, signed on 5/11/16. 3) Petitioner alleges that Decedent was survived by no known next of kin. Declaration filed on 8/27/20 provides due diligence efforts to locate the heirs that are listed in the will. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 8th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 8th amended general order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST AND LAST NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHIA MING SHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/10/2021. The case was opened in the name of the surviving spouse instead of the predeceased spouse. The petitioner has not been appointed as personal representative of the surviving spouse's estate. Notes were e-mailed to counsel on 01/06/2021.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAYMOND JAIR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible due to the recent stay at home order issued by the mayor. Please review the 8th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Petitioner has not filed a response to the Examiner Notes: a. The 10/28/2020 Duties and Liabilities is missing the required telephone number and a new Duties and Liabilities must be filed with all the required information. b. The 11/06/2002 Declaration must be signed and verified by Petitioner. [CRC 7.103] c. Another proof of holographic instrument must be filed with the copy of the instrument attached. Attaching the required attachment to a declaration is not proper procedure.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPH FRANCIS BARROS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible due to the recent stay at home order issued by the mayor. Please review the 8th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The matter set for hearing for the court to determine how to proceed and the court may take the matter off calendar at the hearing. Petitioner has not responded to the following Examiner Notes: a. On page 1 the caption of the Petition Petitioner did not select Authorization to Administer Under the Independent Administration of Estates Act or with limited authority. b. On page 1 under #2c. Petitioner did not select full or limited authority. c. On page 1 under #2d. Petitioner did not select (1) or (2) or (3) d. On page 1 under #3a. Petitioner did state (place) where decedent died. e. On page 2 under 3.d. Petitioner alleges no assets. Please explain why a Petition for Letters of Administration has been filed if there are no assets. f. On page 2 under #3h. Petitioner did not select box 1 or 2 and 3 or 4. g. A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect creditors, even if the will waives, or all heirs waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.37.] h. On page 3 under #5a. Petitioner did not select box 7 or 8. i. Petitioner has not filed Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). j. Petitioner has not filed Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147). k. Petitioner has not filed Proof of Publication. l. A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LISA LYNETTE SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: off calendar

rulings.Case Title: **WILL TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO COUNTY** THE ESTATE OF JOSEFINA G. DIZON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/9/21. Still no courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] Still no proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A., and 14.60 J.] When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? All persons mentioned in a will must be listed in item/attachment 8. If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice must be given as required by Probate Code Section 8113. All courtesy copies, including any papers filed to address the issues noted here, and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/29/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH S. BURNHAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: continued to 2/3/21. notes emailed to counsel on 1/8/21 at 11:57 a.m.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DONALD H. CONDENCIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/02/2021. Issues: 1) The Will does not appear to be original based on routine Examiner observations. Please explain if the Will is an original. [See LRSF 14.20, LRSF 14.23.] 2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 3) Petitioner is not listed in the petition for Probate on page 4 under #8 showing name relationship to decedent, age, and address. 4) A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.7).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ADA DEGHETTO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the purpose of admitting the will dated 6/9/2000. No appearance is required. Status of closing the estate will be set on 7/13/2022. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least one week before 7/13/2022. Appearance will be required on 7/13/2022 if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VILEY ROY BUTLER JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although no determination is made at this time as to whether Skyler King is an heir entitled to any part of this estate. Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 04/20/2022. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 04/27/2022.
